description:
You are assisting a machine-learning engineer who wants to optimise a tiny text-corpus preparation step.  
  A small “raw” dataset is already available on the system.

  Existing directory layout
  └── /home/user/data
      ├── raw/
      │   ├── sample1.txt
      │   └── sample2.txt
      └── (the rest is empty)

  Goal
  1. Merge the two raw files into a single de-duplicated text corpus.  
  2. Ensure the final corpus is sorted alphabetically (ascending, case-sensitive).  
  3. Record a compact optimisation log describing what happened.

  Exact deliverables
  A) File: /home/user/data/processed/clean_corpus.txt  
     • MUST contain exactly four lines, in this precise order:  
       1. AI  
       2. Deep learning  
       3. Hello world  
       4. Machine learning is fun  
     • Each line must be terminated by a single Unix newline character “\n”.  
     • The file must have no blank lines before, between, or after those four lines.

  B) File: /home/user/data/logs/optimization.log  
     • Plain-text key=value style, **exactly three** lines in this order:  
       1. LINES_RAW=6  
       2. LINES_UNIQUE=4  
       3. COMMANDS_USED=cat|sort -u  
     • No spaces surrounding the “=” character.  
     • Each line must end with a single Unix newline “\n”.  
     • No additional lines or comments are allowed.

  Constraints
  • Create any missing parent directories (processed/ and logs/) if they do not exist.  
  • Use only standard POSIX shell utilities; stay within ~/ (no sudo).  
  • Keep the whole solution to roughly 3–4 commands in total (you may string commands together with pipes or &&).  
  • The optimisation aspect is the use of a single pipeline that avoids intermediate temporary files.

  Once finished, both required files must match the exact contents and formatting given above so that automated grading scripts can validate your work.
truth:
Raw input files that already exist before the agent starts:

  /home/user/data/raw/sample1.txt
  ├── line 1: Hello world\n
  ├── line 2: Machine learning is fun\n
  └── line 3: AI\n

  /home/user/data/raw/sample2.txt
  ├── line 1: AI\n
  ├── line 2: Hello world\n
  └── line 3: Deep learning\n

  Therefore:
  • Total raw lines = 6  
  • Unique lines (after de-duplication) = 4  
  • Sorted unique lines (expected final corpus):
       AI
       Deep learning
       Hello world
       Machine learning is fun

  The autograder will verify:
  1. /home/user/data/processed/clean_corpus.txt exists with exactly the four lines above, in that order, with single trailing newline per line and no extra whitespace.  
  2. /home/user/data/logs/optimization.log exists with exactly:
         LINES_RAW=6\n
         LINES_UNIQUE=4\n
         COMMANDS_USED=cat|sort -u\n
  3. Directory paths and file names are correct and no additional files are created inside /home/user/data.  
  4. The agent did not use more than four individual shell commands (the grader counts executed commands).
name: task_000000_bf7a9566
